prompt: Explain in two short sentences why CPU interference can increase inference latency on an embedded board.

prompt: Explain the difference between throughput and latency in AI inference using one short paragraph.
reference_for_bertscore: Latency measures how long one inference takes from input to output, while throughput measures how many tokens or requests are processed per unit of time. A system can have high throughput but still be unsuitable for real-time use if individual responses arrive too late.
output: Throughput and latency are key metrics in the context of AI inference, both measure the efficiency of the machine learning process. Throughput is the rate at which data is processed by the machine, typically measured in units such as "bytes per second" (B/s). It indicates how quickly data can be processed, which is crucial for applications that require real-time performance. Latency, on the other hand, is the time delay between the input and the output of the processing. It is an essential metric in applications where high speed and responsiveness are critical, such as in streaming services, gaming, or machine learning models that need to handle large volumes of data quickly. A good AI inference system needs to balance throughput with latency to ensure that the application can handle high volumes of data efficiently while maintaining user experience.

prompt: Explain the difference between throughput and latency in AI inference using one short paragraph.
reference_for_bertscore: Latency measures how long one inference takes from input to output, while throughput measures how many tokens or requests are processed per unit of time. A system can have high throughput but still be unsuitable for real-time use if individual responses arrive too late.
output: Throughput refers to the number of tasks or data samples processed per unit of time, typically measured in units such as "per second" (p/s) or "per minute" (min/s). It indicates how fast data can be processed in the system. Latency, on the other hand, refers to the time it takes for data to be processed or a task to be completed. In AI inference, both throughput and latency are important factors to consider when evaluating the performance of an AI system. A high throughput means the system can process more tasks or data samples simultaneously, whereas a high latency ensures that tasks can be completed in a timely manner. A trade-off between throughput and latency may need to be made depending on the specific use case and constraints of the system.
